About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: Momentum Ranker Today's data reveals two exceptionally strong performers dominating the momentum rankings. PKX leads with a composite score of 94.6 (rank 1), showing remarkably consistent gains across all timeframes—from 0.8% daily to 91.68% annually—while maintaining perfect SMA alignment and posting a stellar 98.8 resilience score on down SPY days. BELFB follows with extreme long-term appreciation (316% annual return) and massive ratio progression, particularly the 7.97x jump from 3-month to 1-year returns, though its near-flat weekly performance (5.3%) relative to daily strength (1.24%) suggests momentum may be cooling at the shortest horizons. Both securities pass all four ratio consistency checks and trade above their 200-day moving averages, but PKX's tighter ratio progression (14.93 down to 1.06 across timeframes) and superior outperformance versus SPY and QQQ indicate more sustainable, smoother momentum mechanics. The data suggests a market environment favoring large-cap growth and international exposure (note VXUS correlation at 0.65 for PKX), with sharp divergence between these two leaders in their week-to-month return trajectories warranting attention.
